spec_match.rationale: Another simulation facade. The mirrors are regular divs that are manually updated with labels and colors via JavaScript event listeners on select change, bypassing the <selectedcontent> tag entirely.

interactive.rationale: Fully interactive. Picking a theme immediately updates a toolbar chip, a status bar, a breadcrumb, a large preview panel, and a detailed table showing option data.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the customizable select theme-awareness design goal.

edge_cases.rationale: Presents a beautiful simulated preview but doesn't build or mount native customizable select components.

references.rationale: Correctly links to sync-inspector, multiple-mirrors, and mutation-timing.

design_system.rationale: Excellent aesthetic integration with custom chip styles, contrast-compliant colors, and interactive grids.

openQuestions.title: Implement real styled selectedcontent elements
openQuestions.detail: The page should implement native customisable selects and place styled <selectedcontent> descendants in different slots or panels to demonstrate real unstyled DOM clone behaviors.

summary: A highly interactive visual simulator showing how themes can be styled, though built as a JavaScript facade on normal divs rather than real customizable <selectedcontent> tags.
